findings.msg: Many answer cards bake in a specific past offering's clicker/exam key ("Confirmed in lecture — answer C/D", "she flagged as exam-important", "clicker E") rather than the general concept.
findings.fix: Strip the answer-letter/lecture-confirmation tags so cards test curriculum, not one exam's key.

findings.msg: Cards reference specific one-off assignments by number ("In the HW1 gel", "resolves HW1 Q6", "from the discussion sheet"), which only make sense to students who had that exact handout.
findings.fix: Generalize to standalone gel/probability problems; remove HW/discussion-sheet cross-references (see also L1-C008, L2-P016).

findings.msg: Three near-identical 'Band 1 near wells, Band 2 farther, which statement is NOT consistent' multiple-choice cards test the same single fact.
findings.fix: Keep one, delete the other two or repurpose them to a genuinely different figure/skill.

findings.msg: Both ask why the -10 Pribnow box is AT-rich and give the same 2-vs-3-H-bond / easier-to-melt answer.
findings.fix: Merge into one; drop the duplicate.

findings.msg: Both refute 'find the -10 by counting exactly 10 bp upstream' with the identical variable-spacing explanation.
findings.fix: Collapse to a single card.

findings.msg: Says the −35 and −10 elements are 'separated by ~25 bp' — the standard (and exam-tested) spacer is ~17 bp, and this card even contradicts L3-P002, which correctly states 17 bp.
findings.fix: Change spacer to '~17 bp (16–19 bp optimal)'.

findings.msg: Dates the discovery of reverse transcriptase to 1971; Temin and Baltimore both published it in 1970 (Nobel 1975).
Answer says "Plug in the actual primer-binding 8-mer's bases from the discussion sheet" — the card cannot be answered without an external sheet it doesn't contain.
findings.fix: Embed a concrete 8-mer so the problem is self-contained.

findings.msg: Occam's razor "how to pick the best multiple-choice answer" is exam strategy, not molecular-genetics content.
findings.fix: Cut, or recast as a genetics-inference card if kept.

findings.msg: Numerous cards hinge on one lecturer's in-class asides ("Archbold told you to cross out the telomere figure", "she said this is likely on the exam") rather than transferable content.
findings.fix: Convert to the underlying fact (what the textbook figure gets wrong) without the personal framing.

findings.msg: Round×wrinkled Mendelian cross scored on a gel mixes transmission-genetics (a different unit) into the DNA-structure unit, a combo typical of one exam question.
findings.fix: Move to the transmission-genetics deck or replace with a pure fragment-size gel-reading item.

findings.msg: who-what-when is applied to definitional recall cards that carry no scientist or date attribution, diluting the skill tag's meaning.
findings.fix: Drop who-what-when from L3-R004 (+1/TSS), L3-R012 (TBP identity), and L3-C020 (rifampicin mechanism); use mechanism/technique instead.

findings.msg: Tagged 'telomere' but the question is entirely about prokaryotic replication (primers, ligase, single origin, dsDNA) with no telomere content.
findings.fix: Remove telomere; add leading-lagging and/or replication-origin.

findings.msg: Only one tag (foundations) despite an obvious genotype/phenotype-correlation skill dimension.
findings.fix: Add compare-contrast.

findings.msg: L1-R001 and L1-R002 each carry a single tag (foundations), below the 2-tag minimum.
findings.fix: Add a second axis or accept as intentional single-concept definitions and document the exemption.

findings.msg: Three cards all deliver 'reactivating telomerase to cure aging → cancer risk (~80-90% reactivate).'
findings.fix: Retain the concept card plus one problem; cut the third.

findings.msg: Near-identical concept cards giving Southern/Northern/Western targets plus the step unique to each; L1-C009/L1-P007/L1-R023 already cover the same restriction-digest point.
findings.fix: Consolidate the redundant overview cards.

findings.msg: The Griffith/Avery-MacLeod-McCarty/Hershey-Chase discovery story is spread across ~15+ L1 cards (R016-18, C002-04, C017, R043-45, C025-29), far heavier than its likely exam share.
findings.fix: Trim overlapping who-what-when recalls; keep the highest-yield date+contribution set.

findings.msg: Gives 1987 for the discovery of telomerase; Greider & Blackburn identified telomerase activity in Tetrahymena in 1985 (Cell).
Holoenzyme mass '~430 kD' understates it — core ~390 kD plus σ70 (~70 kD) is ~460 kD.
findings.fix: Use ~460 kD, or drop the exact holoenzyme figure.

findings.msg: Very granular names/years untied to molecular mechanism (Carothers 1913 grasshoppers, Astbury & Bell 1938, E.B. Wilson 1895, Brock Yellowstone 1965) read as memorize-the-exam-slide trivia for an intro molecular unit.
findings.fix: Keep the load-bearing history (Griffith/Avery/Hershey-Chase/Watson-Crick); demote the obscure names to optional.

findings.msg: Occam's razor card is tagged only 'foundations' (<2) and is generic test-taking heuristic, not genetics content.
findings.fix: Add a second tag or reclassify out of the genetics decks.

findings.msg: Card explicitly asks which direction small fragments migrate but omits the directionality skill tag.
findings.fix: Add directionality.

findings.msg: 'telomere' tag is a stretch; the card is about the central dogma / reverse transcriptase, mentioning telomerase only as one example enzyme.
findings.fix: Consider dropping telomere; central-dogma+mechanism already cover it.

findings.msg: Both define 'semiconservative'; L2-R001 supersedes it by also naming the conservative/dispersive alternatives.
findings.fix: Cross-tag as a deliberate spiral or drop the barer L1 version.

findings.msg: Three cards restate telomerase = ribonucleoprotein reverse transcriptase (RNA-dependent DNA polymerase, TERT).
findings.fix: Keep one recall + the problem; retire the redundant recall.

findings.msg: States ~10 bp per helical turn while L1-C024 states ~10.5 bp/turn; inconsistent within the deck.
findings.fix: Standardize on 10.5 bp/turn for B-form.

findings.msg: Topically the deck is well-aligned to Unit 1 (structure, replication, transcription); the concern is exam-mirroring granularity, not off-subject content.
findings.fix: Audit for the "one past exam" fingerprint above rather than adding/removing topics.
